titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Felix S. Cohen Papers
titlestmt.author: by Susie R. Bock, Lucy M. Kramer, George Miles, Jennifer Meehan, Stephanie Bredbenner

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday August 27, 2019 at 03:00
Transformed with yale.addEadidUrl.xsl. Adds @url with handle for finding aid. Overwrites @url if already present.
beinecke.cohen.xml converted for compliance with Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines with brbl-migrate-01.xsl (mr2007-08-13).
PUBLIC "-//Yale University::Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library//TEXT (US::CtYBR::::[FELIX S. COHEN PAPERS ])//EN" "cohen.xml" converted from EAD 1.0 to 2002 by v1to02.xsl (sy2003-10-15).
Finding aid revised to reflect comprehensive rearrangement and reprocessing.

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The papers document Felix S. Cohen's professional career as a civil servant, private attorney, law professor, and author. From 1933-1957, Cohen drafted legislation for the Department of Interior, most notably the Wheeler-Howard Indian Reorganization Act of 1934. In 1939 he headed the Indian Law Survey, which compiled all federal laws regarding Native Americans. He edited the summary of that survey, known as The Handbook of Federal Indian Law , which remains a milestone in the evolution of Indian law. Cohen continued to work for Indian and minority interests in private practice in the late 1940s and early 1950s.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The majority of the collection was the gift of Lucy M. Kramer Cohen in 1989 and 1991. "Statutory Compilation of the Indian Law Survey: A Compendium of Federal Laws and Treaties Relating to Indians" (Box 13) and Series VI (Boxes 97-99) were the gift of Arthur Lazarus of the law firm Fried, Frank, Harris, Shriver & Kampelman. The 2007 addition was the gift of Gene Cohen Tweraser and Karen Cohen Holmes, 2007.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Felix S. Cohen Papers are the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Felix S. Cohen Papers. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 59, 102-104 (student records): Restricted until 2028. For further information consult the appropriate curator.
Box 105, folders 1694-1695: Restricted fragile material. Reference surrogates have been substituted in the main files. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 168 (audiotape reels) and Box 170 (dictaphone records): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

bioghist.head: Felix S. Cohen (1907-1953)
bioghist.p: Felix Solomon Cohen (1907-1953) was a lawyer with special interest in natural resources, statehood and economic development for American territories, Indian affairs, immigration, and human rights. He received a B.A. from The City College of New York, an M.A. in philosophy from Harvard in 1927, a Ph.D. from Harvard in 1929, and a J.D. from Columbia Law School in 1931. From 1933 through 1947 he served in the Solicitor's Office of the Interior Department as an assistant solicitor, associate solicitor, and acting solicitor. Cohen drafted the Wheeler-Howard Act (also known as the Indian Reorganization Act) of 1934 and was named chief of the Indian Law Survey in 1939. He edited The Handbook of Federal Indian Law (1941), which remains a milestone in the evolution of Indian law. Cohen reentered private law practice in 1948, and handled several important pro bono cases, including lawsuits which established Native Americans' rights to vote and receive Social Security benefits in states that denied them these rights. Cohen taught at Yale University, The City College of New York, The New School for School Research and the University of Newark (later Rutgers University). He married Lucy M. Kramer in 1931, and they had two daughters. He was an active member of several political and legal organizations, including the Institute of Living Law, the National Lawyers Guild, and the Socialist Party. He died in Washington, D.C. in 1953.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Felix S. Cohen papers document the legal, academic, and scholarly career of Felix S. Cohen. The collection documents Cohen's work as a legislative draftsman and solicitor for the Department of the Interior, including the drafting of the Wheeler-Howard Act (also called the Indian Reorganization Act) of 1934 and the Indians Claims Commission Act and the conduct of an Indian law survey that eventually became The Federal Handbook of Indian Law . Also included are records from Cohen's time as a private attorney, when he won several significant pro bono cases establishing the civil rights of Native Americans, including Trujillo vs. Garley and Harrison vs. Laveen. Cohen's manuscript notes for the oral arguments of Trujillo vs. Garley, of which no printed record exists, are present. Also present are papers documenting Cohen's teaching career at Yale University and City College of New York, as well as his writing projects, notably Ethical Systems and Legal Ideals . Additional papers document Cohen's work with several political and legal organizations, including the Socialist Party and the Institute of Living Law. Finally, the collection includes personal and family papers.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The 2007 addition was first processed in 2007-2008. It initially received a separate finding aid with the call number WA MSS S-2602. The two collections (WA MSS S-1325 and WA MSS S-2602) were reprocessed and combined in 2019, retaining the earlier call number. Box 170, folders 2333-2335 are unused. Original audiotape reels are housed in box 168. Restricted fragile material.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into ten series: I. Legal Career, 1931-1953. II. Teaching Career, 1946-1953. III. Writing Projects, 1916-1953. IV. Organizational Affiliations, 1935-1953. V. Personal Papers, 1933-1992. VI. Arthur Lazarus Addition, 1928-1954. VII. Oversize Papers, 1930-1950. VIII. Restricted Papers. IX. Restricted Fragile Papers, 1947-1953. X. 2007 Addition, 1927-1960.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Original folder titles have been maintained. Supplied titles and additional title information are indicated by square brackets.

subject.part: Indians of North America -- Government relations -- 1934-
Indians of North America -- Legal status, laws, etc.

persname.part: Cohen, Felix S., 1907-1953 ( Combating totalitarian propaganda: a legal reappraisal)
Cohen, Felix S., 1907-1953 ( Felix S. Cohen's Handbook of federal Indian law)
Cohen, Felix S., 1907-1953 ( Readings in jurisprudence and legal philosophy)

corpname.part: United States. Department of the Interior
United States. Office of Indian Affairs
College of the City of New York (1926-1961)
New York Association for New Americans

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series consists of released office files from Cohen's years with the Solicitor's Office of the Department of the Interior (1933-47) and from his private practice (1948-53).

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into six subseries: Indian Affairs, Indian Law Survey, Alaska: Natural Resources and Native Affairs, Territories, Department of the Interior Solicitor's Office Records, and Private Practice Office Records.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains Department of the Interior files documenting the implementation of the Indian Reorganization Act of 1934 (known originally as the Wheeler-Howard Act) and routine legal work concerning the department's Indian policies. The files consist of internal memoranda; correspondence with outside experts, other government agencies, legislators, and private interest groups; drafts of reports and legal opinions; printed bills and drafts; and printed material by government agencies and private institutions. The files also contain articles written by Cohen, with supporting documentation, connected with his work for the department.

did.unittitle: Las Casas, etc. [notes on early Spanish writings regarding Indian land rights]
Powers of the Commissioner [of Indian Affairs]
How Long Will Indian Constitutions Last?
Constitution - making [correspondence regarding article "How Long Will Indian Constitutions Last?"]

The New Trail. I-VII [examination of the Wheeler-Howard Act]
The New Trail [: conferences with tribes regarding the Wheeler-Howard Act]
Indians on a New Trail - misc.
Law and order 1904-1935 [regulations on Indian reservations]
Law and order regulations - draft and report
Revision of regulations [for the Indian Service]
Probate Regulations (revised) [due to Wheeler-Howard Act, re: inheritance]
Election regulations [reorganized under the Wheeler-Howard Act]
Power of Dept. over grazing regulations
Notes on legal powers of tribe
Legal Powers of an Indian Tribe Memoranda

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These files document the work of the Indian Law Survey, a joint effort by the Departments of Justice and Interior. The results of the survey were later published as The Handbook of Federal Indian Law .

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five groupings: Reports, Memoranda and Correspondence, Compilation, Handbook of Federal Indian Law Drafts, etc., and Subject Files.

did.unittitle: Status reports to Littell and/or Collett
Material for Handbook : [inter-department memoranda, reports, pages from compilation]
Material for Handbook : [inter-department memoranda, reports, pages from compilation]
Fabian, Robert H. [chairman of Advisory Committee on Indian Lawbook]
Mulroney, Mr. [chief of the Trial Section]
"Statutory Compilation of the Indian Law Survey: A Compendium of Federal Laws and Treaties Relating to Indians:"
Handbook of Federal Indian Law , Drafts, etc.

p.ref: See also: Series III. Writing Projects for 1941 and 1982 editions, Box 71-72, folders 1142-43

did.unittitle: Page proofs, 1940 edition published by Government Printing Office
Draft and comments of chp. 9, Individual rights in tribal property; chp. 12, Indian education; chp. 25, Pueblos of New Mexico
Draft and comments of chp. 9, Individual rights in tribal property; chp. 12, Indian education; chp. 25, Pueblos of New Mexico

Review 1945 edition in Yale Law Journal
Dept. of Justice Style and Citation Manual for the Lands Division
Eminent domain book, sections relating to Indians
Alaska: Natural Resources and Native Affairs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries documents Cohen's work for the Interior Department on Alaskan Indian issues, concerning natural resources, and aboriginal rights. Some of these files were used and augmented by Cohen while in private practice.

did.unittitle: George W. Folta, [Counsel at Large, Dept. of Interior, Juneau Office]

relatedmaterial.head: Related Archival Materials note
relatedmaterial.p: See also: Oversize, Box 100, folder 1617, Box 101, folder 1636

did.unittitle: Indian Office - briefs in Alaska fishery hearings
Alaska native hunting and fishing rights
Alaska salmon trap bill - S. 1446; H.R. 3859
[Letters to legislators introducing Alaskan development bill]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The following files were arranged by Cohen under the title "Current Alaska Material." If he used the title to designate files he was currently using, it may reflect his legal interests just prior to his death.

did.unittitle: Alaska - [Cohen's] letter to [Washington] Post on Timber Road
Metlakatla [Indian Community] and Annette Islands Reserve
Alaska fishing case (Paul case) and condemnation case
Tlingit - Haida [draft memorandum of law, Possessory Rights of Tlingit and Haida Indians]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains Cohen's Department of Interior files concerning issues involving United States territories. Files consist primarily of draft reports and memoranda, press releases, and other documents by the department. The problems Cohen worked on include immigration of World War II refugees to United States territories, such as the Virgin Islands, establishing the status of the territory of Guam, and the human and natural resources of Puerto Rico.

did.unittitle: ["Philippine Islands: Economic Conference," press release by the Department of State]
Newspaper Clippings - "Science and Politics in Plans for Puerto Rico"
PR 1934 on [articles and notes by Cohen and research material]
Agricultural Company case, Suarez- Martinez v. Tugwell: clippings
PR Agricultural Co. case 1946 - pleadings, etc.
Complete inventory of housing properties to be taken over by Puerto Rico Housing Authority
Puerto Rico hearings - House and Senate
Puerto Rico - self-government (drafts, duplicates, notes, miscellaneous papers)
Puerto Rico ["Legislative Reorganization in Puerto Rico: A Call and a Program"]
Draft regulations - V.I. - admission of aliens to V.I.
Report to Secretary, Nov 18 - Virgin Islands
Department of the Interior Solicitor's Office Records

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into ten groupings: Aliens - Immigrants, Atomic Energy, Patents, Public Lands and Natural Resources, Reclamation, Spanish Embargo of 1937, Other Issues, Annual Reports and Correspondence, Structure of Interior - Relations with Other Units, and Personnel Files.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The subseries documents Cohen's work on issues not included in the preceding subseries, including immigrants, atomic energy, and public lands and natural resources. Materials comprise memoranda, correspondence, reports, and departmental press releases.

Official Status - Felix S. Cohen, Department of Interior

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This section contains material from Cohen's private practice beginning in 1948. Eventually Cohen's office became the Washington, D.C. branch of the New York law firm Riegelman, Strasser, Schwarz and Spiegelberg. Additional files with material from this period can be found in the previous topical subsections of this series. Files doucment Cohen's work on cases concerning Native Americans and immigrants, including his work for the American Jewish Committee and New York Association for New Americans. Also included are Cohen's timesheets and daily notes from his work on particular cases.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The files have been arranged alphabetically by Cohen's folder titles, usually by name of client or legal associate.

did.unittitle: Dodge, Thomas H. [superintendent Truxton Canaon Agency, Hualapai Indians]

scopecontent.p: Sent to Lucy M. Kramer in 1982 by Fried, Frank, Harris, Shriver & Kampelman

did.unittitle: Wilkinson, Ernest L. [attorney Cohen worked with]
Wright, C. M. [attorney Cohen worked with]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II documents Felix S. Cohen's courses at the Yale Law School (1946-53) and in the Philosophy Department of the City College of New York (1948-53). The files include correspondence with the departments about running the course, letters to students, student papers, Cohen's lecture notes, and final exams.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three groupings: Material for Yale and CCNY, Yale Courses, and CCNY Courses. Because Cohen taught jurisprudence at both institutions, lecture notes which could not be specifically assigned to either Yale or CCNY have been placed at the beginning of the series. The files have been arranged by university, course, and semester

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Several folders of student recirds were restricted and moved from boxes 60 and 61 to box 59 (in the restricted papers series) during reprocessing, 2019.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains reviews, articles, addresses, books, and pamphlets by Cohen, as well as notes and background material.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into six groupings: General, Book Reviews, Articles, Addresses, Books and Pamphlets, and Works by Others. Within each grouping, titles are arranged alphabetically.

scopecontent.p: Similar correspondence for 1935-40, see Box 41, folder 1209, and for 1939-50, see Boxes 57-58, folders 511-519

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Institute of Living Law and Other Organizations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Files documenting Felix S. Cohen's association with the Institute of Living Law and several private, non-profit action groups and professional institutions. The files generally contain publications by and about the institutions and information on meetings and membership.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three groupings: General, Legislative Projects, and "Handbook of Propaganda Warfare."

did.unittitle: First edition published in law reviews
Combatting Totalitarian Propaganda: A Legal Reappraisal . Washington, D.C.: Institute of Living Law (two copies)
Draft forward to Combatting Totalitarian Propaganda: A Legal Reappraisal
Draft of "Handbook of Propaganda Warfare," the work as originally planned by the Institute
Review, sale, receipts of Combating Totalitarian Propaganda: A Legal Reappraisal
Review, sale, receipts of Combating Totalitarian Propaganda: A Legal Reappraisal

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged by organization.

did.unittitle: National Lawyers Guild: Committee on International Law
National Lawyers Guild and arms embargo
National Lawyers Guild: correspondence re: Spanish Report
National Lawyers Guild: history of Spanish report

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series is organized into two subseries: Correspondence and Cohen Family Material, in which material is organized under individual's names. Correspondents with less than five letters have been placed in "Letter" general files.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Communication with Joseph Blau, Huntington Cairns, Elizabeth Carus, The Day , William Hirsch, Mark DeWolfe Howe, Jerome Hyman, Ernest Nagel, Lester M. Nichols, Louis and Jane Roth, Charles Stein, Sidney Wallach, and Philip P. Wiener concerns Morris R. Cohen's writings. Correspondence with Eliot Cohen, Huntington Cairns, Sidney Hook, Samuel Merwin, Jerome Michael, Ernest Nagel, Lester M. Nichols, and Morris Opler focuses on Felix S. Cohen's articles. Sidney Ratner and Roscoe H. Vining ask Cohen to address various groups, while Daniel J. Bronstein, Ruth Calvin, Humbert Cofrances, Harold L. Ickes, Marjorie Merritt, Virginia Mishnun, Richard Powell, and Emanuel Rackman sought Cohen's advice on various legal questions or his help in their careers.

Many friends and colleagues Cohen corresponded with were interested in Indian affairs. People such as Ruth Muskrat Bronson, William A. Brophy, Dennis Chavez (Senator from New Mexico), Henry Cohen, Chester Faris, William Gilbert, Theodore H. Haas, John B. Hart, Harold L. Ickes, Alexander Lesser, Norman Littell, Richard Powell, Sister Providencia, Harry N. Rosenfield, and Howard M. Schott shared their opinions on Indian affairs with Cohen or sought out Cohen for his expertise. Most of the files also contain personal letters and several concern family business.

title.part: Journal of the History of Ideas

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains material given to Lucy M. Kramer by H. N. Rosenfield

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into three groupings: Felix S. Cohen and Lucy M. Kramer, Morris R. Cohen, and Leonora C. and Harry N. Rosenfield

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The files for Felix S. Cohen and Lucy M. Kramer (Box 90-95) have a great deal of biographical information. The files also reflect their socio-political interests. The Morris R. Cohen files (Box 95-96) relate primarily to his written works. The Leonora C. and Harry N. Rosenfield files (Box 96) consist of material about Felix S. Cohen and Morris R. Cohen.

did.unittitle: Felix S. Cohen and Lucy M. Kramer
Awards, Honors, Memorial Services for Cohen

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers in Series VI are Felix S. Cohen's files, created when he worked for the Interior Department, released and brought by him into private practice.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two groupings: Indian Constitutions and Other Material. Within groupings, files are arranged alphabetically.

did.unittitle: Chippewa bands of Wis. and Mich. [constitutions]

title.part: Hearings...on...HR 4453...to Prohibit Discrimination in employment because of race, color, religion, or national origin

did.unittitle: Indian Delegation Act [right of Secretary of the Interior to delegate power to the Commissioner of Indian Affairs]
Newspaper articles on sale of Indian land intertribal policy
US Constitution, etc. [address by Harold L. Ickes]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains oversize material from Series I, III, IV, and V. The arrangement is based on the size of the material, but it also parallels the order of the collection.

did.unittitle: Department of the Interior. Commission to the Five Civilized Tribes. Map Showing Progress of Allotment in Cherokee Nation
Alaska: Natural Resources and Native Affairs
"Inventory of United States Land Resources and their Use - 1500-1950," chart
Indians on a New Trail - Misc
Page proofs, 1940 edition published by Government Printing Office
Analytical Index of Federal Indian Law

Alaska General - "Cohen Raps Alaska Colonialism: Government, Industry Both Blamed," Alaska Federationist v. 3, no. 15 (Juneau, Alaska)
National Lawyers Guild and arms embargo
Alaska: Natural Resources and Native Affairs
Alaska Development Program: 1946, "What's Wrong with Alaska?, The Washington Post
Agricultural Company Case, Suarez- Martinez v. Tugwell: Clippings
Budget survey [recommendations for structure of legal work in Interior Dept.]
Official Status - Felix S. Cohen Department of Interior
"Ethics of Our Foreign Policy," University of Texas Great Issues Class
"Noble Red Man - American Indians Are Tired of Being Treated Like Irresponsible Children," by Fred Theroux
Cohen Family Material - Felix S. Cohen and Lucy M. Kramer
Cohen Family Material - Morris R. Cohen

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 59, 102-104: Restricted until 2028. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Restricted Fragile Papers, contains fragile originals. The material is arranged in folder order.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference surrogates have been substituted in the main files. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The addition was first processed in 2007-2008. It initially received a separate finding aid with the call number WA MSS S-2602. Arrangement was largely carried out in accordance with how the initial collection (Felix S. Cohen Papers, WA MSS S-1325) was arranged. Files arrived with no discernable order and extensive re-organization was required to arrange files into series and subseries. Descriptive information at the file level is based on a basic survey of the folder contents. Descriptive information about the collection as a whole was drawn in part from the finding aid for the Felix S. Cohen Papers, WA MSS S-1325. The two collections (WA MSS S-1325 and WA MSS S-2602) were reprocessed and combined in 2019, retaining the earlier call number.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Gene Cohen Tweraser and Karen Cohen Holmes, 2007.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four subseries: I. Legal Career, 1930-1955. II. Writing Projects, 1927-1960. III. Organizational Affiliations, circa 1927-1940. IV. Personal Papers, 1920-1953.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The material documents aspects of Cohen's legal career as a civil servant, private attorney, and author, as well as his social, political, and professional activities and personal and family life. Included in the addition are files stemming from Cohen's work as a legislative draftsman and solicitor for the Department of the Interior, relating especially to Indian affairs and natural resources, including the drafting of the Wheeler-Howard Act and the Indian Claims Commission Act. Also included are papers concerning his work as a private attorney after he left government service in 1947, relating to Indian affairs, immigration, and voting rights, including his manuscript notes for oral arguments in the cases of Trujillo vs. Garley and Harrison vs. Laveen (1948). It also includes files relating to Cohen's various writing projects, including Ethical Systems and Legal Ideals (1933) and The Legal Conscience (1960); and extensive files stemming from his activities with the Socialist Party and the National Lawyers Guild and other social, political, and legal organizations (circa 1930-1940), including the League for Industrial Democracy and the Institute for Living Law. Also present are some personal and family papers.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: The addition consists of material that was stored in the basement and other areas of the Cohen residence in Washington, D. C. and that came to light after the death of Lucy Kramer Cohen in 2007.

appraisal.head: Appraisal
appraisal.p: One box of materials labeled "Lucy Kramer Cohen papers" was removed and added to the Lucy Kramer Cohen papers (WA MSS S-2635) in 2019.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Legal Career subseries consists of files stemming from Cohen's work with the Solicitor's Office of the Department of the Interior from 1933 to 1947, with his private practice from 1948 to 1953, and Cohen's early legal career, including law school.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The Legal Career subseries is arranged into four groupings: Indian Affairs, Department of the Interior Solicitor's Office Records, Private Practice Office Records, and Other Professional Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concerns the Department of Interior files largely documenting the drafting and implementation of the Indian Reorganization Act of 1934 (IRA, known originally as the Wheeler-Howard Act), a 1939-1940 survey of Indian Law that resulted in the publication of The Handbook of Federal Indian Law, and routine legal work concerning the department's Indian policies. The files consist of internal memoranda, correspondence, drafts of reports and legal opinions, printed bills and drafts, and printed material by government agencies and private institutions.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into four groupings: Wheeler-Howard Act, Indian Law Survey, Indian Claims Commission Act, and Other Indian Affairs Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains drafts, memoranda, printed materials, and other papers concerning the drafting and implementation of the Wheeler-Howard Act (also called the Indian Reorganization Act) of 1934.

did.unittitle: Preliminary drafts of Indian Self-Government Bill
Wheeler-Howard Bill File, drafts and correspondence
Wheeler-Howard Indian Bill, Correspondence, charter recommendations, atlas, publicity
General Notes on Proposed Legislation - Siegel, Shepard, Marshall
Department Report on Wheeler-Howard Indian Rights Bill
Hearings and testimony on Wheeler-Howard Act
Official Indian Vote on Wheeler-Howard Bill
Congressional Bills on Indian Self-Government, Drafts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Files stemming from a joint project of the Department of the Interior and Department of Justice, on which Cohen served as chief, to create a file of federal legislation and treaties concerning Native Americans. The project resulted in an internal publication entitled "Statutory Compilation of the Indian Law Survey: A Compendium of Federal Laws and Treaties Relating to Indians" and The Handbook of Federal Indian Law . The files consist primarily of memoranda, research material, and drafts.

did.unittitle: Department of Justice Memoranda - Application Memos
Chap. 3 Indian Treaties, Chap. 9 Individual Rights in Tribal Prop., Chap. 10 The Rights of the Indian in his Personality
Lists of Court Cases Regarding Indian Affairs
Handbook of Federal Indian Law, Preliminary drafts, Chapters 1 and 3

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts, revisions, notes, and printed material concerning the Indian Claims Commission Act, circa 1940-1946.

did.unittitle: Sec. 2(c) Coverage of Act - Classes of Cases
Sec. 2(g) Groups that can sue as plaintiffs
Sec. 9 Annual Report of Commission (Deleted from later versions, not in final act)
Sec. 10(g) Groups that can sue as plaintiffs (Presentation of Claims)
Sec. 11 Transfer of Suits from Court of Claims
Sec. 14 Investigations (Calls upon the Departments for Information)
Sec 14 (E) Evidence and Proof (Calls upon Departments for Information)
Sec. 16 No Member of Congress to Practice before Commission
Sec. 21 (F) Objections to Determination (Deleted, not in final version)
Sec. 21 Report of Commission to Congress
Sec. 23 Dissolution of Commission (Term of Office)
Sec. 24 (C) Coverage of Act; Classes of Cases
"Legislative Material on the Indian Claims Commission Act of 1946"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, memoranda, and printed materials concerning Indian Affairs, 1930-1942.

did.unittitle: Office of Indian Affairs Radio Programs
Hearings, Reports and Speeches in Congress
Department of the Interior Solicitor's Office Records

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Files relating to Cohen's work with the Solicitor's Office of the Department of the Interior on issues pertaining to natural resources, including government control of coal mines during the Second World War and the use of helium as an energy source. The files consist of memoranda, correspondence, drafts of reports and legal opinions, and printed material. Also present are various government publications.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into three groupings: Coal Files, Helium Files, and Printed Material. Files within each grouping are arranged in rough chronological order.

did.unittitle: Regulations for the Operations of Coal Mines Under Government Control

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Files stemming from Cohen's private legal practice relating to Indian affairs, immigration, and voting rights. Of particular note are Cohen's manuscript notes for his oral argument, of which no printed record exists, in the case of Trujillo vs. Garley, an Indian voting rights case in New Mexico.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Files are arranged in rough chronological order.

did.unittitle: Trujillo vs. Garley and Harrison vs. Laveen (New Mexico and Arizona voting rights) with notes
Trujillo vs. Garley, New Mexico's Voting Case - Manuscript Notes for Argument
Summary Analysis of Proposed Omnibus Immigration Bill
Briefs to Supreme Court of New York County
Joint Efforts General - Tabulations on Cases
Non-Joint Efforts - Office Memos, Briefs, etc.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in rough chronological order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reference files, memoranda, correspondence, and printed material broadly related to Cohen's professional activities as a lawyer. Material overlaps with that found elsewhere in the Legal Career subseries.

did.unittitle: [Correspondence, map, and telephone directory from Felix S. Cohen's desk]
Material to be read by F.S.C
Paper for 13th Conference on Science, Philosophy, and Religion
Papers from Conference on Moral Standards
French Report on U. S. Indians
Other Papers, Memoranda, Notes, and Reports
Paper, incl. some summaries of white attitudes towards Indians
Tangential material incl. Papers on Indian Tribes, Model Constitutions, and Contents of Mrs. Springer's article

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into seven groupings: Articles and Reviews, Ethical Systems and Legal Ideals, Indian Land Values, Lectures, The Legal Conscience, Writings by Others, and Printed Material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes, research material, manuscripts, drafts, and correspondence relating to articles and book reviews by Cohen that appeared in various law journals and relating to his book, Ethical Systems and Legal Ideals, which was originally published in 1933; drafts for a work on Indian land values; notes and drafts for lectures on various topics; and correspondence and source material for the posthumous compilation of Cohen's writings entitled The Legal Conscience , edited by Lucy Kramer Cohen, which was published in 1960. Also found are scattered writings by others, and offprints and publications of writings by and about Cohen.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in rough chronological order.

did.unittitle: Review of Boudin, "Government by Judiciary" - notes, manuscript
Review of Ehrlich, "Fundamental Principles of the Sociology of Law"
"The Relativity of Philosophical Systems and the Method of SystematicRelativism"
"What City College Will Contribute to the Development of Law"
Readings in Jurisprudence and Legal Philosophy
Readings in Jurisprudence and Legal Philosophy
Encyclopedia articles on casuistry and Lorimer, James
"A New Philosophy for a New Age" and "A Lawyer's Picture of Capitalism"
Labor, The Constitution and the Courts
Conference on Methods in Philosophy of the Sciences
"Ethics and Morals of Our Foreign Policy"
Source Material, I. Law and Ethics
Source Material, I. Law and Ethics
Source Material, II. The Indian's Quest for Justice
Source Material, III. Studies in the Philosophy of American Democracy
Source Material, III. Studies in the Philosophy of American Democracy
"The Aesthetic and Metaphysical Bases of the Conflict of Laws"
"Felix S. Cohen: A Fighter for Justice"
A Jurisprudential Symposium in Memory of Felix S. Cohen, Rutgers Law Review

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Social and Political Organizations, and Legal Organizations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Organization Affiliations series consists of material stemming from Cohen's participation and involvement with various social, political, and professional organizations. In particular, there are extensive files relating to Cohen's activities with the Socialist Party and the National Lawyer's Guild.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into two groupings: Socialist Party and Other Social and Political Organizations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries largely consists of files relating to Cohen's involvement with the Socialist Party during the 1930s. Also present are files relating to his activities with the League for Industrial Democracy, the Alumni Committee on Academic Freedom at City College, the American Civil Liberties Union, and other social and political organizations. The files contain memoranda, correspondence, notes, report, minutes, publications, and other printed material.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged as received.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, memoranda, reports, and publications concerning Cohen's involvement with the Socialist Party, 1927-1945. Includes Cohen's notes from party conferences, correspondence, and reports from the Socialist Party Finance Committee, Socialist News Bureau, and the Socialist Party National Affairs Committee. Includes issues of The Socialist and newspaper clippings concerning the Socialist Party.

did.unittitle: 1935 Conference - "We the First Socialist Government of the United States"

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Files are arranged by organization with printed material arranged at the end.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Files relating to Cohen's involvement with other social and political organizations, including the League for Industrial Democracy, the Alumni Committee on Academic Freedom at City College, and the American Civil Liberties Union. The files contain memoranda, correspondence, notes,report, minutes, publications, and other printed material.

did.unittitle: L.I.D. [League for Industrial Democracy] F.S.C. Misc
Student League for Industrial Democracy Student Outlook Looking Forward
League for Industrial Democracy - Announcements, Monographed Materials, etc.
Saposs, D. - American Labor History - Outline
Labor Dept. and League for Industrial Rights
Pacifism - Committee on Militarism in Education
Alumni Committee on Academic Freedom, City College
Continental Congress of Workers and Farmers
Social Science Research Council, "Report to the Social Science Research Council on the Organization of a Program in the Field of Personality and Culture"
The American Choice by Henry A. Wallace
[Social and Political Organizations - Brochures, Reports, and Other Printed Material]
[Social and Political Organizations - Newspapers and Newsletters]
[Social and Political Organizations - Newspapers and Newsletters]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Files relating primarily to Cohen's activities with the National Lawyers Guild, where he served on the International Law Committee and National Executive Committee, as well as some files relating to his work the Intitute of Living Law. The files contain reports, memoranda, correspondence, research material, publications, and other printed material.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into two groupings: National Lawyers Guild and Institute of Living Law.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged as received.

did.unittitle: Lawyers Guild - Letter of Resignation, etc.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged as received.

did.unittitle: "Hitler's Imperialistic Goals in Connection with Nazi Activity in the United States"
[Institute of Living Law Envelope from F.S.C. files, with annotations]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Student papers, family scrapbooks, photographs, reel-to-reel tapes, and other papers documenting Cohen's personal and family life.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in rough chronological order.

did.unittitle: Clipping, Article on Felix S. Cohen in City College Campus Paper
Draft of Resignation Letter from Solicitor's Office and Clipping
Death certificate of Felix S. Cohen (copy)
Felix S. Cohen Service, Washington, D.C.
